source: Gordon et al., 2001, Cancer Research; Kramer et al., 2007, Wiley; Mourad et al., PMC5785435; Bevacizumab SmPC; Safety meta-analysis PMC3227908; PF-06439535 biosimilar study, Regulatory Toxicology and Pharmacology, 2018
description: Expected findings for anti-VEGF and anti-VEGFR monoclonal antibodies (bevacizumab class). VEGF-A is essential for vascular maintenance, glomerular integrity, and wound healing. Cynomolgus monkey VEGF is 99% homologous to human VEGF. Inherits Fc-mediated base findings from general_mab.

expected_findings.description: Proteinuria (glomerular endothelial permeability from VEGF blockade)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3 (nephrotic, >3.5 g/24h) = adverse. Renal insufficiency = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: VEGF regulates glomerular endothelial permeability (podocyte fenestration). Dose-dependent increase (22-38% with bevacizumab). (Kramer et al., 2007, Wiley; Bevacizumab SmPC)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Major hemorrhage (GI, pulmonary, intracranial) = adverse. Fatal hemorrhage = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: VEGF maintains vascular integrity. Blockade disrupts this producing capillary fragility, petechiae, and epistaxis. (Sandler et al., 2006; Bevacizumab SmPC)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Major organ hemorrhage (pulmonary, intracranial) = adverse. Fatal hemorrhage = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Microscopic correlate of vascular integrity disruption from VEGF blockade. (Bevacizumab SmPC)

expected_findings.description: Physeal dysplasia in juvenile animals (impaired endochondral ossification)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Progressive epiphyseal changes = adverse. Severe skeletal dysplasia = never reclassify.
expected_findings.species_note: Critical for pediatric risk assessment. Documented in juvenile bevacizumab studies.
expected_findings.rationale: VEGF is essential for endochondral ossification and bone remodeling. Anti-VEGF produces physis dysplasia in growing animals. (Gordon et al., 2001, Cancer Research)

expected_findings.species_note: Female animals only. VEGF required for follicular development and corpus luteum formation.
expected_findings.rationale: VEGF is required for follicular angiogenesis and corpus luteum formation. Anti-VEGF disrupts reproductive organ angiogenesis. (Bevacizumab reproductive toxicology)
Adrenal cortex is highly vascularized; VEGF blockade may reduce cortical vascularity. (Bevacizumab nonclinical studies)

expected_findings.severity_threshold: TMA or progressive glomerulopathy = adverse. Frank renal failure = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Severe VEGF inhibition produces TMA-like changes in the kidney; reflects dose-dependent adversity continuum. (Bevacizumab SmPC)

expected_findings.description: Wound healing impairment at surgical/incision sites
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Non-healing wound with infection or wound dehiscence = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: VEGF is essential for granulation tissue neovascularization in wound repair. Anti-VEGF should be withheld 28 days pre/post surgery. (Bevacizumab SmPC)
